competitions.title: 2026 International Residential Award
competitions.description: Flagship international award celebrating built and unbuilt residential projects of all scales, with categories for single-family, multi-family, and affordable housing.
competitions.terms_summary: Open to licensed architecture firms worldwide. Early-bird entries receive a discounted fee and extended upload period. Built projects must be completed after January 1, 2020.

competitions.title: 2026 Emerging Residential Voices (Student)
competitions.description: International student competition exploring experimental housing typologies that respond to climate, culture, and community.
competitions.terms_summary: Open only to current architecture and urban design students (undergraduate or graduate). Team entries of up to 4 members are allowed. Digital submissions only.

competitions.title: 2025 Student Urban Courtyards Challenge
competitions.description: Student-only ideas competition for reimagining underused inner courtyards as inclusive public spaces in dense European cities.
competitions.terms_summary: Open to enrolled students in architecture, landscape, or urbanism programs. Anonymous submissions required. Prize pool distributed among three jury-selected winners.

competitions.title: 2025 Campus Resilience Studio
competitions.description: Design competition for resilient and adaptable educational environments on university campuses, focusing on climate adaptation and community use.
competitions.terms_summary: Individual or team student entries accepted. Entries must include site analysis, phasing strategy, and resilience measures. Jury includes campus planners and sustainability experts.

competitions.title: 2025 Global Cultural Landmark Award
competitions.description: Award recognizing exemplary museums, galleries, and cultural centers that redefine public engagement with art and heritage.
competitions.terms_summary: Open to built cultural projects completed between 2019 and 2024. Multiple project entries allowed per firm. Jury focuses on community impact and visitor experience.

competitions.title: 2025 Asia-Pacific Hotel Innovation Awards
competitions.description: Regional award spotlighting innovative hotel and resort architecture across the Asia-Pacific region, with an emphasis on guest experience and cultural integration.
competitions.terms_summary: Open to hospitality projects completed after January 1, 2018, located in Asia-Pacific. Entries may be submitted by architects, interior designers, or hotel operators with owner consent.

competitions.title: 2024 People's Park Revitalization Award
competitions.description: Award celebrating built public space projects that have successfully revitalized urban parks and plazas through community-driven design.
competitions.terms_summary: Open to both professional firms and student-led design-build studios. Projects must demonstrate at least one year of public use and community engagement.

competitions.title: 2024 International Education Campus Award
competitions.description: Global award highlighting exemplary campuses, schools, and learning environments that advance pedagogy and community engagement.
competitions.terms_summary: Eligible projects include K-12 schools, universities, and vocational campuses. Entries judged on spatial flexibility, daylighting, and inclusivity.

competitions.title: 2023 Small Homes, Big Ideas
competitions.description: Competition for compact residential designs demonstrating high-quality living within limited footprints, including micro-apartments and tiny homes.
competitions.terms_summary: Open to professionals and students. Both conceptual and built projects accepted. Submissions evaluated on spatial efficiency and environmental performance.

competitions.title: 2023 Student Social Housing Studio
competitions.description: Student design competition focused on inclusive and climate-responsive social housing in rapidly growing Latin American cities.
competitions.terms_summary: Only individual student submissions accepted. Proposals must address affordability, community spaces, and passive climate strategies.

competitions.title: 2026 Urban Public Realm Futures
competitions.description: International award for visionary designs transforming streets, squares, and waterfronts into resilient and people-centered public realms.
competitions.terms_summary: Open to interdisciplinary teams including architects, landscape architects, and urban designers. Both realized and speculative projects may be submitted.

competitions.title: 2026 Global Cultural Campus Award
competitions.description: Award recognizing cultural campuses and museum districts that integrate architecture, landscape, and public programming at an urban scale.
competitions.terms_summary: Open to built cultural precincts and multi-building campuses. Entries must demonstrate phased implementation and strong wayfinding strategies.

competitions.title: 2022 Adaptive Reuse Prize
competitions.description: Prize honoring transformative adaptive reuse projects across all building types, from industrial heritage to modern office retrofits.
competitions.terms_summary: Eligible projects must retain at least 50% of the existing structure. Submissions evaluated on carbon savings and cultural significance.

competitions.title: 2025 Nordic Housing Accessibility Challenge
competitions.description: Design challenge for inclusive and accessible multi-family housing in Nordic climates, emphasizing universal design and social integration.
competitions.terms_summary: Open to architecture practices based in or collaborating with partners in Nordic countries. Built and unbuilt proposals accepted with detailed access strategies.

competitions.title: 2025 Student Pavilion Micro-Competition
competitions.description: Rapid-brief student competition for temporary cultural pavilions that can be assembled and disassembled with minimal waste.
competitions.terms_summary: Open to current students worldwide. Designs limited to 50 sqm footprint and must consider reuse or recycling of all materials.

competitions.title: 2026 Next-Gen Hotel Concepts
competitions.description: International competition for visionary hotel concepts that rethink guest experience, operations, and sustainability in an era of climate and demographic change.
competitions.terms_summary: Open to architecture and interior design firms. Conceptual projects welcome; proposals must include operational and sustainability narratives.

projects.title: Sky Courtyard Residences
projects.subtitle: Passive house-inspired urban infill housing
awards_detail: 2025 Nordic Housing Accessibility Challenge – Special Mention
2023 Small Homes, Big Ideas – Urban Infill Category Winner
projects.description: A mid-rise residential block organized around a series of stacked courtyards, combining cross-ventilated apartments with shared terraces and winter gardens. The project meets passive house standards and prioritizes accessibility for all residents.

projects.title: Vertical Garden Lofts
projects.subtitle: Green façades for high-density living
awards_detail: 2023 Small Homes, Big Ideas – High-Rise Shortlist
projects.description: A compact tower of loft apartments wrapped in layered planting and operable shading. The project integrates greywater recycling, on-site food production, and shared roof gardens to create a vertical neighborhood.

projects.title: Riverbank Timber Homes
projects.subtitle: Mass-timber riverside community
awards_detail: 2023 Small Homes, Big Ideas – Community Housing Shortlist
projects.description: A cluster of mid-rise timber buildings arranged along a restored riverbank, combining affordable family units with co-housing clusters. Elevated walkways and planted decks protect against seasonal flooding.

projects.title: Desert Courtyard Villas
projects.subtitle: Low-rise villas around shaded courts
projects.description: A series of private villas organized around shared shaded courtyards and narrow lanes. The project reinterprets vernacular patterns for contemporary resort living on the edge of the city.

projects.title: Bamboo Horizon Resort
projects.subtitle: Eco-resort embedded in rice terraces
awards_detail: 2025 Asia-Pacific Hotel Innovation Awards – Eco-Resort of the Year
2024 Adaptive Reuse Prize – Shortlist (Landscape Integration)
Regional Tourism Board Design Excellence Award
projects.description: An all-bamboo resort cascading down a terraced hillside, with open-air suites and pavilions connected by gently sloping pathways. Natural ventilation, on-site agriculture, and low-impact foundations minimize environmental footprint.

projects.title: Skyline Courtyard Hotel, Shanghai
projects.subtitle: Urban hotel with stacked sky courts
awards_detail: 2025 Asia-Pacific Hotel Innovation Awards – Urban Hotel Winner
City Hospitality Design Award
projects.description: A 22-storey courtyard hotel that weaves planted voids and elevated gardens through the tower volume. Generous public terraces extend the street life vertically, while a high-performance façade reduces cooling loads.

projects.title: Fjord Light Hotel
projects.subtitle: Cliffside retreat over the fjord
awards_detail: 2025 Asia-Pacific Hotel Innovation Awards – International Guest Experience Shortlist
projects.description: An 18-storey hotel stepping down a rocky slope, with every room oriented to capture fjord views and northern light. Shared lounges and spa terraces are carved into the cliff, creating a sequence of sheltered outdoor spaces.

projects.title: City Layers Civic Gallery
projects.subtitle: A porous cultural podium for the city
awards_detail: 2025 Global Cultural Landmark Award – Emerging Gallery Winner
2022 Adaptive Reuse Prize – Civic Category Winner
projects.description: A compact cultural building that stitches together existing street levels, tram lines, and riverfront promenades into a stepped public podium. Flexible galleries, civic rooms, and a rooftop garden share a single interconnected volume.

projects.title: Harborlight Museum Annex
projects.subtitle: Waterfront expansion for a maritime museum
awards_detail: 2025 Global Cultural Landmark Award – Jury Prize
2024 Adaptive Reuse Prize – Shortlist
European Waterfront Renewal Award
projects.description: An annex for the city’s maritime museum that floats between refurbished warehouses and the river. The project combines exhibition decks, a public pier, and an outdoor amphitheater overlooking the harbor.

projects.title: The Listening Theatre
projects.subtitle: Black-box performance space carved into a slope
awards_detail: 2025 Global Cultural Landmark Award – Performance Space Shortlist
projects.description: A compact theatre embedded in a landscaped slope, with public steps forming an informal amphitheatre above the main performance hall. The building doubles as a civic lookout and outdoor stage.

projects.title: Northern Lights Learning Center
projects.subtitle: Arctic campus for lifelong learning
awards_detail: 2024 International Education Campus Award – Shortlisted
projects.description: A compact campus in the Arctic circle, arranged as a ring of classrooms and workshops around a sheltered winter garden. The building generates more energy than it consumes and becomes a community hub after school hours.

projects.title: Terraced Hillside School
projects.subtitle: Outdoor learning on a stepped landscape
awards_detail: 2024 International Education Campus Award – Learning Environments Shortlist
projects.description: A primary school cascading down a wooded hillside, with outdoor terraces doubling as classrooms, play decks, and community gathering spaces. Lightweight roofs and deep overhangs provide shade and channel rainwater.

projects.title: Oakridge Community College
projects.subtitle: A porous campus woven into the neighborhood
awards_detail: 2024 International Education Campus Award – Community Campus Shortlist
projects.description: A community college campus that opens its library, workshops, and sports facilities to the surrounding neighborhood. A central learning street connects a series of timber-framed pavilions.

projects.title: Riverside Community Boardwalk
projects.subtitle: Reclaimed industrial edge as public promenade
awards_detail: 2024 People's Park Revitalization Award – Jury Winner
projects.description: A continuous timber boardwalk that transforms a formerly industrial river edge into an accessible public promenade, with play decks, wetlands, and informal stages along the way.

projects.title: Skyline Rooftop Park
projects.subtitle: Elevated linear park above the city
awards_detail: 2024 People's Park Revitalization Award – High-Density Public Realm Winner
Urban Design Excellence Award
projects.description: A former service roof converted into a continuous public garden weaving between towers, with lookout decks, play zones, and urban agriculture plots open to the public.

projects.title: Desert Shade Plaza
projects.subtitle: Canopied civic square for a hot climate
awards_detail: 2024 People's Park Revitalization Award – Climate-Responsive Shortlist
projects.description: A central civic square sheltered by a perforated canopy that filters sunlight and channels breezes. Mist fountains, planted mounds, and shaded seating support all-day use.

projects.title: Harborfront Steps
projects.subtitle: Amphitheatre and tidal edge for the waterfront
projects.description: A broad flight of steps and terraces that mediates between the city’s main boulevard and the tidal harbor edge, hosting events, daily lunch breaks, and informal recreation.

projects.title: Metropolitan Grand Plaza
projects.subtitle: Reorganized civic heart for a dense downtown
awards_detail: 2024 People's Park Revitalization Award – Large Plaza Winner
Regional Landscape Architecture Award
projects.description: A multi-level civic plaza reorganizing a complex intersection of transit lines and office towers into a legible public room, with gardens, fountains, and performance spaces.
